input:
Dear Mom and Dad,

I got here and the bus ride was SO long. My cabin is called Birch and there are 7 other kids in it. My bunk is on top which is cool but kind of scary. The counselor's name is Jake and he seems nice. There are trees everywhere and I can hear the lake from our cabin. I already unpacked my stuff but I forgot my flashlight. Can you mail it?

Love,
Sophie

Dear Mom,

I don't like it here. The bed is hard and it's cold at night and I can hear weird noises outside. I miss my room and I miss Pepper. Is she sleeping on my bed? She probably misses me too. The other kids are fine but they all seem to already know each other from last year. Can you call the camp and ask if I can come home early? I know you said to give it a week but a week is really long.

Love,
Emma

Dear Mom and Dad,

CAMPFIRE COOKING NIGHT. We didn't just roast marshmallows. We actually cooked dinner on the fire. Foil packets with chicken and potatoes and corn. You put them right on the coals and wait. Mine got a little burnt on the bottom but the rest was amazing. Everything tastes better outside. That's just a fact. Then we had banana boats for dessert which is a banana with chocolate chips wrapped in foil. So good.

Love,
Emma

Dear Mom,

Our cabin has a thing now where every night before bed someone tells a story. Not a scary one, just a story. Last night Marcus told one about a fish who wanted to climb a mountain. It made no sense but we were all laughing so hard. Tonight it's my turn and I have no idea what to say. Sophie said I should tell the one about when our dog ate the Thanksgiving turkey. Can I?

Miss you,
Tyler

Dear Mom,

The food here is interesting. Breakfast is always OK because it's hard to mess up cereal and toast. But lunch today was something called "campfire stew" and I don't want to talk about it. Dinner was better, they did tacos and you could put your own stuff on. The bug juice is this weird red drink that tastes like melted popsicles. Everyone drinks it though. Please send goldfish crackers and those granola bars, the chocolate chip ones. I'm serious.

Love,
Sophie

Dear Mom and Dad,

It has been raining for two days straight. Two. Days. We can't go to the lake or do archery or anything outside. We played board games until I never want to see Uno again. Marcus taught us this card game but he keeps changing the rules when he's losing. The cabin smells like wet socks. Jake says the sun is coming back tomorrow but I've heard that before.

Love,
Sophie

Dear Mom and Dad,

I have to tell you something and I want to say it right. I think I'm different than when I got here. Not like a totally different person. But the edges are different. Like I'm the same shape but smoother or something. I don't get as nervous about new things. I talk to people I don't know. I tried food I'd never eat at home. I'm not sure when it happened. There wasn't one moment. It was all of them added up.

Miss you,
Mia

Dear Dad,

Everyone is writing in each other's journals. I let Tyler write in mine and he drew a cartoon of our whole cabin with everyone's names. It's really good. He even drew the spider that lived in the corner that we named Gerald. I hope Gerald is OK after we leave.

Love,
Jordan
